picks.title: One Useful Thing — AI as a Thinking Partner
picks.note: The most grounded writing on what AI actually changes about knowledge work. Mollick avoids both hype and dismissal — useful framing for anyone building with or alongside AI tools.

picks.title: Berkshire Hathaway — The Full Story
picks.note: Compounding, patience, and decision-making under uncertainty told as a 50-year company story. Maps cleanly onto options thinking — time is the asset most people undervalue.

picks.title: The AI Canon
picks.note: Curated reading list for understanding large language models from first principles. Worth revisiting as the models keep moving — the fundamentals hold even when the benchmarks shift.

picks.title: Working Backwards
picks.note: Amazon's internal product process — press release technique, six-pagers, bar-raiser hiring — told by two insiders. The PRFAQ format alone is worth the read.

picks.title: Howard Marks Memos — Risk and Uncertainty
picks.note: The best writing on second-order thinking and calibrated uncertainty in markets. Read alongside anything options-related and the complementary concepts become hard to unsee.

picks.title: Shreyas Doshi on Strategic Thinking for PMs
picks.note: The clearest thinker on what separates senior PM work from execution-mode PM work. His framing on upstream vs downstream thinking has stuck with me.
